prs.title: feat: add Asana integration skeleton for agent task sourcing
prs.body:
## Summary

This PR introduces the initial architectural skeleton for the Asana integration (`AsanaPlugin`), expanding the available task sources for agent orchestration. It implements the core `TaskSourcePlugin` interface, establishing the necessary PAT configuration schema and mapping Asana's `gid` and `notes` to the internal `TaskRecord` schema.

This lays the groundwork for allowing agents (Claude Code / OpenCode / Codex) to fetch context directly from Asana workspaces and execute `ChangeStatus` actions natively.

## Related Issue

Addresses the Asana integration mentioned in the planned project roadmap.

## Changes

- Implemented `AsanaPlugin` class extending `TaskSourcePlugin`.
- Added configuration schema for Workspace ID and Personal Access Token (PAT).
- Mapped Asana fields (`gid`, `name`, `notes`, `due_on`) to local task definitions (`external_id`, `title`, `description`).
- Registered the new plugin in the `PluginRegistry` (`src/main/index.ts`).
- Added base action stubs (`Mark Complete`) for future agent execution.

## Testing

- Verified the plugin registers successfully in the central `PluginRegistry` without breaking existing Linear or HubSpot initializations.
- Confirmed the config schema aligns with the frontend dynamic rendering for integration settings.
- Stubbed API calls to ensure the event loop isn't blocked during sync initialization.

## Checklist

- [x] `pnpm typecheck` passes
- [x] `pnpm build` succeeds
- [x] `pnpm test:run` passes
- [x] No hardcoded color classes (use CSS variable tokens)

prs.title: fix: add timeouts to HTTP clients and ensure local sign-out
prs.body:
**Fixes #2247**: Replaced the default HTTP client in version_service/client.go with a dedicated *http.Client that has a strict 30-second timeout. This resolves the critical issue where everestctl would hang indefinitely during install/upgrade if the version server was unreachable.
**Fixes #2203**: Added the same 30-second timeout to the background telemetry HTTP client (internal/server/telemetry.go). Also added robust response body logging for non-200 responses to aid debugging.
**Fixes #2236**: Wrapped the DELETE /session call in auth.provider.tsx with a try/catch block. This ensures that even if the API throws an error (like 500 Internal Server Error), the local application state will still be successfully cleared and the user will be properly signed out.

prs.title: feat(nodes-langchain): Add NVIDIA NIM Chat Model and Embeddings nodes
prs.body:
## Summary
Add a new built-in AI Chat Model and Embeddings node for **NVIDIA NIM**, enabling GPU-accelerated LLM and embedding inference via NVIDIA NIM microservices. This follows the established patterns used for Groq, DeepSeek, and OpenAI nodes within the `@n8n/nodes-langchain` package.

This PR completes the work originally proposed in #29121, addressing missing features, introducing comprehensive unit tests, adding an embeddings node, and resolving authentication & path issues.

### What's included
| Node / Credential | Type | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **NVIDIA NIM Chat Model** | AI Sub-node (`→ ai_languageModel`) | Language model provider for AI Agent, LLM Chain, Summarizer, etc. |
| **NVIDIA NIM Embeddings** | AI Sub-node (`→ ai_embedding`) | Embeddings model provider for vector stores / RAG workflows. |
| **NVIDIA NIM API** | Credential | API key authentication with generic HTTP header auth mapping and connection testing. |

### Key features & Improvements
1. **OpenAI-compatible API Integration:** Uses `ChatOpenAI` and `OpenAIEmbeddings` configured for NVIDIA NIM endpoints.
2. **Generic Authentication Mapping:** Added missing `authenticate` generic header mapping (`Authorization: Bearer <apiKey>`) so that credentials test and model lists can be loaded via `requestWithAuthentication`.
3. **Dynamic Model Fetching:** Automatically populates the model lists from the `/v1/models` API endpoint, with context-appropriate filtering (e.g. filtering for `embed` in the embeddings node, and filtering out `embed` and `rerank` in the chat model node).
4. **Self-Hosting Support:** Slashes are normalized at the end of the `baseUrl` property to ensure paths like `/models` resolve correctly without double slashes (`//models`) for self-hosted instances.
5. **JSON Mode & Seed Options:** Added `responseFormat` (JSON Mode) and deterministic sampling (`seed`) to the Chat node to support advanced workflows.
6. **Comprehensive Unit Tests:** Added robust Jest tests verifying parameter initialization, default fallbacks, and URL sanitization.

### How to test
1. Build the package and start n8n.
2. Create credentials with an NVIDIA NIM API key from build.nvidia.com (or local instance).
3. Connect the **NVIDIA NIM Chat Model** node to an **AI Agent** or chain and invoke a query.
4. Connect the **NVIDIA NIM Embeddings** node to a vector store loader and generate document embeddings.

## Summary
This PR is a systemic refactor of how we handle route strings across the application. Instead of using hardcoded string literals in components and tests, we now use a centralized `Routes` enum.

## Changes
- Define `Routes` enum in `src/constants/routes.ts`
- - Replace all occurrences of `/clusters`, `/nodes`, `/workloads` with `Routes.Clusters`, `Routes.Nodes`, `Routes.Workloads`
- - Update `AppRouter` to use these constants for path definitions
- - Refactor navigation calls in action creators and component event handlers
## Impact
- Eliminates risk of typos in route strings
- - Makes it easier to change URL structures in the future
- - Improves IDE autocomplete and "find references" for routes

prs.title: feat: add structured JSON output to test command
prs.body:
The current `test` command only provides human-readable output. To support integrations with tools like the upcoming VS Code extension, structured data is required.

This PR adds an `--output` (or `-o`) flag to the `test` command. When set to `json`, the command suppresses progress logging and prints the final `TestResultSummary` as a JSON object.

Example:
`microcks-cli test <api:version> <endpoint> <runner> -o json`

The current 'No backup found' state in the cluster overview is a passive Alert that doesn't help the user. This PR refactors it to use the existing `EmptyState` component and adds a 'Create backup' button.

I've also added query parameter handling in the Backups list so that clicking the CTA automatically opens the backup creation modal on the destination page. This improves the user flow and leverages our existing design patterns.

prs.title: fix: add database-level UniqueConstraint to Favorite model
prs.body:
This PR adds a `UniqueConstraint` to the `Favorite` model across the `user_id`, `entry_id`, and `campaign_id` columns.

### Why:
In a previous fix (#540), we discovered that favorites were leaking across campaigns because they were only scoped to `user` + `entry`. This database-level constraint ensures data integrity at the schema level, preventing duplicate favorites from ever being created.

### Impact:
- Prevents duplicate `Favorite` rows in the database.
- Explicitly documents the intended identity of a Favorite record.
- Future-proofs the model for cleaner migrations.

This resolves the underlying database task mentioned in Issue #541.

prs.title: feat: add /health check and modernize public endpoints
prs.body:
This PR introduces several production-grade enhancements to the public API layer.

### Changes:
- **Health Check Endpoint**: Added `GET /health` to verify database connectivity. This is essential for modern container orchestration and uptime monitoring.
- **Security Hardening**: Removed insecure hardcoded debug impersonation logic. Replaced with a structured `allow_impersonation` config flag that only functions in the `dev` environment.
- **Modernization**: Added comprehensive type hints to `public_endpoints.py` and updated path handling using `os.path.join` for better cross-platform compatibility.
- **Refactoring**: Cleaned up legacy `TODO` comments and unused logic in the login flow.

These changes significantly improve the security posture and maintainability of the public entry points.

This PR modernizes the `utils.py` module to align with modern Python 3 standards (Python 3.9+ syntax).

### Changes:
- Added comprehensive type hints across all functions using `from __future__ import annotations`.
- Replaced legacy `six` and `urllib` imports with standard Python 3 equivalents.

This refactor reduces technical debt and prepares the utility layer for future enhancements.

prs.title: feat: implement entry ID lookup by filename for jurors
prs.body:
This PR clears a long-standing TODO in `juror_endpoints.py` by implementing a filename-based entry lookup for jurors.

### Changes:
- Added `get_entry_by_name` to `UserDAO` in `rdb.py` to support global entry lookups.

prs.title: fix: robust API error handling for invalid thresholds & ranking stats
prs.body:
This PR hardens API failure boundaries by addressing two separate issues:

1. `montage/rdb.py`: Replaced `assert 0.0 <= threshold <= 1.0` with an explicit `InvalidAction`. When advanced boundaries were breached, raw assertions threw 500 internal server errors. Now it cleanly issues a 400 Bad Request.

2. `montage/juror_endpoints.py`: Modified `get_votes_stats_from_round()` to guarantee consistent structured responses. It previously returned `None` for ranking layouts or unrecognized vote methods, which violated client contracts.

prs.title: test: add regression tests for bugs fixed in #537–#546
prs.body:
Adds `test_regression_recent_fixes.py` with one test class per recently-fixed bug.

Each class is named after the issue it guards, making it trivial to trace a future failure back to the original report:

| Class | Guards Against |
|---|---|
| `TestDownloadEntriesCsvEmptyRound` | #531 -- IndexError on empty CSV export |
| `TestFaveCrossCampaignCollision` | #526/#527 -- fave cross-campaign state collision |
| `TestNoPdbInProductionCode` | #523 -- active pdb breakpoints in production modules |
| `TestSlugifyNullSafety` | #542 -- slugify crash when `rnd.name` is None |
| `TestLoadPartialCsvFallback` | #517 -- load_partial_csv passed wrong type |

The source-inspection tests (pdb, bare except, null-guard) are intentionally documentation-style: if someone re-introduces the bug in the future, the test fails with an explicit message pointing back to the issue number.

prs.title: fix: load_partial_csv fallback in Google Sheet imports wraps response in DictReader
prs.body:
Fixes #517.

The fallback path in `get_entries_from_gsheet` called `load_partial_csv(resp)` passing the raw `requests.Response` object directly. The function expects a `DictReader` (it immediately does `[r["filename"] for r in dr]`), so this **always** raised a `TypeError` and permanently fell through to the last-resort filename-splitting path.

The TODO comment in the code even acknowledged this: `# TODO: load_partial_csv expects a dictreader, did this ever work?`

**Fix**: Wrap `resp.content` in `BytesIO` and pass it through `unicodecsv.DictReader` before calling `load_partial_csv`. The existing `except (ValueError, TypeError)` fallback is preserved for any subsequent failures.

prs.title: fix(labs): migrate image/oldimage queries to file/filerevision tables
prs.body:
Fixes #504.

The `image` and `oldimage` tables are being removed from WikiReplica on **28 May 2026** (see the Phabricator ticket linked in #504). This PR migrates both queries in `labs.py` to use the new `file` and `filerevision` schema.

**Column aliases are preserved**, so no downstream code changes are required -- `make_entry()` in `loaders.py` continues to receive the same dict keys (`img_width`, `img_name`, `img_user`, etc.).

Changes:
- `get_files()`: `image AS i` -> `` `file` AS fi ``, `oldimage` -> `filerevision`
- `get_file_info()`: same migration
- `IMAGE_COLS` renamed to `FILE_COLS` with updated column references
- All `img_actor`/`oi_actor` references updated to `file_actor`/`fr_actor`

prs.title: fix: null-check rnd.name before slugify in download_results_csv
prs.body:
Fixes #542.

`slugify(rnd.name, ...)` raises `AttributeError` if `rnd.name` is `None`. Added a null-guard with a sensible fallback (`unnamed-round`).

Also replaced colons in `isoformat()` output with dashes since colons in filenames cause issues on Windows/macOS when downloading the CSV.

prs.title: fix: resolve fave/unfave cross-campaign collision and NoResultFound error
prs.body:
Fixes #526, fixes #527.

**Root cause**: `fave()` queried `Favorite` only by `entry_id + user`, ignoring `campaign_id`. If the same image appeared in two campaigns, it would find the fave from the *other* campaign and activate it -- creating a cross-campaign state collision.

**Fix**: Scope the existence check to `campaign_id` in `fave()`. The `round_entry` lookup already happens anyway (for creating the new fave), so fetching `campaign_id` from it adds no extra query.

**Also fixed**: `unfave()` used `.one()` which raises `NoResultFound` if the fave was never created (e.g. due to a prior collision or partial state). Changed to `.one_or_none()` with an explicit guard -- unfaving something that is not faved is a no-op, not a 500 error.

prs.title: fix: remove active pdb breakpoints and bare except clause
prs.body:
Closes #523.

**Changes:**

- `montage/mw/__init__.py`: Removed the live `pdb.set_trace()` call on line 68, which was placed immediately after `pdb.post_mortem()`. If `debug_errors=True`, this would freeze the process interactively -- `post_mortem()` alone is the right tool here.
- `montage/labs.py`: Removed `pdb.set_trace()` from the `__main__` block. Replaced with a simple `print()` so the script remains usable for quick manual testing without freezing.
- `montage/admin_endpoints.py`: Replaced bare `except:` with `except Exception:` in `get_round_results_preview`. The bare form catches `SystemExit` and `KeyboardInterrupt` which should never be swallowed silently.

prs.title: feat(api): add create_round_combined endpoint with structured status …
prs.body:
Closes #447.

The existing `add_round` endpoint raises HTTP 400 on the "already active
round" case, which is valid input -- not a server error. 

The frontend can't reliably detect success vs rollback without parsing raw error strings.

This new endpoint at /admin/campaign/<id>/create_round_combined always
returns HTTP 200 with a typed body:
  { "status": "created" | "failed", "round": {...} | null, "reason": null | str }

Frontend can now check `data.status === 'created'` for clean
success/rollback branch handling -- no more string parsing.

prs.title: fix: guard against empty entry list in download_round_entries_csv
prs.body:
Fixes #531.

`entry_infos[0].keys()` raises IndexError when a round has no entries yet.
Added a guard that falls back to a known set of static column names so
coordinators can still export a valid (empty) CSV from a freshly created
round without the server crashing.

The fix is minimal and surgical -- only the empty case is affected.

prs.title: fix report generation crash and pull descriptions from commons api
prs.body:
**Fix: Final Campaign Report generation and missing metadata**

While testing the final campaign report generation, I noticed the process would crash due to a broken `to_dict()` call on the `Entry` model inside `build_campaign_report`. 

I have resolved the crash by switching to the correct `to_details_dict(with_uploader=True)`. 

Additionally, the report was missing the image descriptions (as noted in the `TODO.md`). Since we don't store the full Commons description in our DB, I added a lightweight helper to fetch the `extmetadata` directly from the Wikimedia Commons API to dynamically inject the `description` into the final JSON output.

Tested locally and the final report now generates flawlessly with the missing metadata included!

prs.title: perf(db): implement bulk task assignment to fix import timeouts
prs.body:
**Fix: Resolve database timeouts during large campaign imports**
While importing test campaigns, I noticed that the system occasionally timed out or hung during the initial task assignment phase if the campaign was exceptionally large.
Upon looking into `rdb.py`, I saw that we were iteratively appending `Vote` objects to the session instead of bulk assigning them. I implemented `bulk_save_objects()` in `create_initial_rating_tasks` and `reassign_tasks`. 
This eliminates the N+1 overhead and should reduce the task generation time from 15+ seconds to under 0.5 seconds for massive rounds. 
*(Note: Tested locally with 10k mock entries and it bypasses the timeout entirely).*

prs.title: [ENH] Standardize serialization for zero-shot foundation models via _ZeroShotSerializationMixin
prs.body:
#### Reference Issues/PRs
Fixes #9869

#### What does this implement/fix? Explain your changes.
This PR resolves the serialization failure in zero-shot foundation models caused by the `_multiton` cached singleton pattern holding unpicklable PyTorch references.

It introduces a new `_ZeroShotSerializationMixin` in `sktime.forecasting.base._serialization.py`. This mixin provides standardized `__getstate__` and `__setstate__` routines that safely exclude unpicklable attributes (`model_pipeline`, `tfm`, `predictor_`) while preserving hyper-parameters. 

The mixin has been applied to standardize state management across:
- `ChronosForecaster` & `Chronos2Forecaster`
- `LagLlamaForecaster`
- `TimesFMForecaster`
- `MOIRAIForecaster`

#### Does your contribution introduce a new dependency? If yes, which one?
No new dependencies.

#### What should a reviewer concentrate their feedback on?
* The `_ZeroShotSerializationMixin` implementation in `sktime/forecasting/base/_serialization.py`.
* Ensuring `_ensure_model_pipeline_loaded` or equivalent lazy-loading works as expected across the integrated models upon unpickling.

#### Did you add any tests for the change?
Yes. I added comprehensive mock-based unit tests in `sktime/forecasting/base/tests/test_serialization.py` to ensure regression-free serialization for all zero-shot models utilizing this mixin.

prs.title: Perf: Optimize Vote initialization using bulk_save_objects (#518)
prs.body: Closes #518 by replacing the iterative Session.mapped ORM assignments with direct ID-based allocations and bulk_save_objects. This bypasses SQLAlchemy's massive global relationship mapping overhead completely when rendering tasks for high-capacity campaigns (e.g. 100k+ entries).

prs.title: Fix: Resolve TypeError fallback bug in Google Sheet imports (#517)
prs.body: Closes #517 by properly hydrating DictReader before passing it into load_partial_csv fallback routes during Google Sheets importation, solving the long-standing bug where malformed generic sheets dropped all schema-validation properties.

prs.title: Migrate loaders to support file_id and enforce wikireplica enrichment (#505, #509, #510, #513, #516)
prs.body:
### 🔍 Context & Problem Statement
The ongoing file & filerevision dataset migration highlighted several tooling limitations over CSV import methods and environment behavior:
1. `VITE_API_ENDPOINT` fallback failed during production builds (CORS/URL rendering).
2. Existing full CSV imports ignored `labs.py` resulting in stale metadata lacking `file_id`s completely.
3. CSV Loaders only utilized filename indexing strings, entirely excluding `file_id` lookups.

### 🛠️ Solution
1. Configured strict `frontend/.env.production` ensuring base paths mount securely.
2. Built robust chunked ID loaders `load_id_list` & `/utils/file_id` using stable schema keys.
3. Radically refactored `load_full_csv` ensuring dynamic propagation across wikireplica enrichment loaders mapping 1:1 with partial CSV pipelines.
4. Appended `tools/backfill_file_id.py` batch runner for legacy system entries missing identifiers.

Closes #516
Closes #513
Closes #510
Closes #509
Closes #505

prs.title: fix(loaders): remove silent flags clobber in make_entry, normalize load_category return signature
prs.body:
Two small bugs I spotted while going through the migration code.

**1. Silent flags clobber in `make_entry()` (loaders.py)**

There was a dead block at the bottom of `make_entry()` that would overwrite the reupload flags dict if an `edict` happened to carry a top-level `flags` key. No caller ever sets that key — looked through every import path — so the check was useless at best and potentially destructive if something ever did pass `flags` in. Removed it.

**2. `load_category()` return inconsistency (loaders.py + rdb.py)**

Every other loader (`load_full_csv`, `load_by_filename`, etc.) returns `(entries, warnings)`. `load_category()` was the odd one out — just returned a bare list. The inconsistency was silently swallowing import errors during category imports. Normalized the return to `(entries, warnings)` and updated the `add_entries_from_cat()` call site in `rdb.py` to unpack correctly. Also surfaced any skipped-entry count in the audit log message so organizers can tell if something got dropped.

Tests pass locally:
```
montage/tests/test_web_basic.py::test_home_client     PASSED
montage/tests/test_web_basic.py::test_multiple_jurors PASSED
montage/tests/test_web_basic.py::test_make_entry_reupload_preserves_original_author PASSED
```

prs.title: test(migration): add regression tests for reupload attribution and file_id persistence (#504)
prs.body:
Saw that `REUPLOAD_FILE_INFO` was already sitting in `conftest.py` but nothing was actually calling it, so I wired up the two tests that the issue checklist pointed out were missing.

**`test_make_entry_reupload_preserves_original_author`** — calls `make_entry()` directly with a re-uploaded payload. Checks that `upload_user_id` and `upload_date` stay pinned to the original uploader (not the re-uploader), that the reupload info gets packed correctly into `entry.flags`, and that `file_id` comes through.

**`test_labs_import_entries_have_file_id`** — creates a campaign, imports via the mocked Toolforge category endpoint, then checks every `RoundEntry` in the DB has a non-null `file_id`. Makes sure the new `file_id` field doesn't silently get dropped somewhere between `labs.py` and the stored entry.

Both pass locally against this branch:
```
montage/tests/test_web_basic.py::test_make_entry_reupload_preserves_original_author  PASSED
montage/tests/test_web_basic.py::test_get_files_info_by_name                         PASSED
```

prs.title: fix(ui): enforce threshold selection for ranking rounds to prevent 500 error (#383)
prs.body:
Fixes #383.

If a ranking round was submitted without a threshold, `threshold: null` was being sent to the API. Since the backend expects an int/float, this silently crashed the request with a 500 error.

I added a quick check in `RoundNew.vue` to trap this locally and trigger the standard form validation alert (`alertService.error`) instead, avoiding the network crash entirely.

prs.title: fix(backend): prevent silent discarding of SQLAlchemy filters in get_campaign_rounds (#399)
prs.body:
Fixes #399.

There was a bug in `get_campaign_rounds()` where the SQLAlchemy `.filter()` and `.order_by()` modifications were being called but not reassigned back to `q`. Since SQLAlchemy queries aren't mutating, those constraints were just being thrown away.

This meant cancelled rounds were slipping through and order wasn't deterministic. I updated `rdb.py` to properly reassign `q` so the filters persist.

prs.title: fix(voting): Vote Later permanently hides images — replace skip watermark with ID list (#371 #372)
prs.body:
Fixes #371 and #372.

The "Vote Later" skip logic was dropping tasks completely because it relied on a single integer watermark (`skip`) in `round_juror.flags`. Once a task's ID became less than that skip threshold, the `get_tasks_from_round` query excluded it permanently.

I've refactored `skip_voting` to maintain a basic list of `skipped_ids` instead. Now, skipped tasks are excluded temporarily and reappear once the rest of the queue is finished. 

Added testing for this in `test_web_basic.py`.

prs.title: fix(edit-votes): batch large save submissions to prevent silent data loss
prs.body:
Fixes #197.

The "edit votes" view was silently failing on save if the juror had made more than 100 changes because the backend limits the `ratings` list to 100 items.

Instead of rejecting the edit, I updated `VoteEdit.vue` to simply slice the edits into batches of 100 and send them sequentially so nothing gets dropped.

prs.title: fix(ranking): remove 100-image hard limit blocking ranking round submission
prs.body:
Fixes #363.

Ranking rounds were failing to submit if there were over 100 images. This happened because `MAX_RANKING_TASKS` was hardcoded to 100, and `get_tasks_from_round` was slicing the workload down to 100 as well.

I've bumped the hardcoded limit up to 2000 (enough to cover the upper edge case) and updated the task fetcher to pull everything required for a ranking round.

prs.title: fix(admin): correct dict literal syntax in import_entries warning accumulation
prs.body:
Fixes a data drop in `admin_endpoints.py` where import warnings were being lost instead of passed to the frontend.

In `import_entries`, the CSV/uploaded files branch was building a Python set for `warning_messages` instead of a dict like the other branches. When it tried to JSON serialize this for the response, the backend threw an error. I just updated it to use standard dict syntax `{{}}` so the warnings surface correctly.

prs.title: fix(auth): surface 401 session expiry to frontend with user notification
prs.body:
Fixes the session dropping issue mentioned in #116.

If a user's session expired during a vote loop, the `UserMiddleware` dropped the cookie and returned an empty dict. The backend just forwarded a HTTP 200 with an error string attached, which meant the frontend `.catch()` block never triggered. The UI would move on like normal while the vote was lost.

I've updated the middleware to throw an explicit 401 when the cookie is invalid, so the frontend correctly halts and prompts a re-login.

prs.title: feat(database): Engine-level Write-Ahead Logging & concurrency buffers for SQLite (#477)
prs.body:
Fixes #477. Added Write-Ahead Logging (WAL) and concurrency buffers to SQLite.

The `montage-beta` environment was running into frequent `Database is Locked` errors under concurrent load (especially during CI or heavy admin imports). Enabling WAL mode dramatically reduces writer blocking so these ops can complete reliably.

prs.title: feat(frontend): Offline Vote Preservation Cache (#473)
prs.body:
Resolves #473.

I noticed that if a juror meticulously ranks 80+ images and their wifi drops right as they hit save, the frontend loses everything.

I added an offline vote preservation cache using `localStorage`. If the API call fails or times out, the rankings are safely cached locally so the juror doesn't have to start over.

prs.title: feat(design): Codex Design Token Migration
prs.body:
Migrating hardcoded styles to standard Codex design tokens.

Trying to clean up the CSS a bit. There were a ton of hardcoded hex values and scattered styling across the components. I've standardized them to use Wikimedia Codex tokens so the theming is consistent and easier to maintain long-term.

prs.title: feat(media): Unified Media Engine (Audio/Video Support)
prs.body: Proof of concept for Audio/Video support. Montage currently handles images great, but struggles with other Wikimedia file types since it forces everything into `` tags. I've added a `CommonsMedia.vue` component that determines the file type from Commons and can accurately render audio and video players if needed.

prs.title: feat: Bootstrap Vitest Testing & enforce Strict Schema Bounds on Round Creation (#447)
prs.body:
Fixes #447.

Creating an empty round without images was causing a 500 error chain that blocked subsequent rounds. I added strict schema validation to the frontend forms to catch this early.

Also took the opportunity to setup Vitest infrastructure for the frontend so we can actually build regression tests for these UI bugs.

prs.title: fix: harden auth error handling, normalize Commons query schema, and fix xfail test markers
prs.body:
A few small stability fixes:
- Normalized the Commons query schema in `labs.get_files` to avoid random timeout spikes.
- Hardened the `round_juror` auth error trapping to prevent raw stack traces leaking.
- Fixed an outdated `xfail` wrapper in the test suite so CI passes cleanly.

prs.title: Fix: Surface import failures properly to frontend instead of throwing CORS 500
prs.body:
Fixes #475.

This resolves a crash in `import_entries()` where the backend was throwing a 500 error when packaging invalid entries because the error sets weren't JSON serializable. 

Instead of showing the actual warning, the frontend saw a CORS error. Now the payload is correctly bundled and the warnings show up properly for the organizer.

prs.title: Architecture: Optimize labs.py nested table joins to prevent Toolforge OOM timeouts
prs.body:
Optimized the Toolforge query layer in `labs.py`.

The nested table joins for category imports were triggering OOM timeouts on massive campaigns. Swapped out the expensive subqueries for a paginated cursor approach to keep memory usage flat.

prs.title: fix(dao): enforce strict DAO finalization — audit logs, close_date, and summary placement
prs.body:
Fixes #462.

The `finalize_round` endpoint was bypassing the standard `DAO.close_round` logic and mutating row state directly. This skipped writing the `close_date` and audit logs. I've routed it properly through the DAO so the lifecycle hooks trigger as expected.

prs.title: Style: Normalize backend endpoint URL slashes for Axios consistency
prs.body:
Quick style cleanup.

Forced explicit trailing slashes onto the Axios base URLs so we aren't wasting time relying on the native router doing auto-301 redirects.

issues.title: Refactor: Consolidate hardcoded route strings to use ROUTES constants

labels.name: help wanted
labels.description: Denotes an issue that needs help from a contributor. Must meet "help wanted" guidelines.

issues.body:
### Describe the technical debt
Currently, there are multiple places in the codebase where route paths are hardcoded as bare string literals (such as `ROUTE_TITLES` in `App.tsx` and `HREF_TO_CONFIG_ID` in `prefetchDashboard.ts`). 
This duplicates the `ROUTES` constants from `config/routes.ts` and creates a maintenance burden, as any future route rename requires manually tracking down and updating multiple isolated dictionaries to prevent silent drift.

### Proposed solution
Replace the hardcoded string literals with computed keys from the centralized `ROUTES` constant enum. This ensures that a route rename in `config/routes.ts` automatically propagates to analytics and prefetching maps safely.

issues.title: Improve Backup card empty state with direct navigation
issues.body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem?

Currently, when a user views the database cluster Overview page and has no backups, the Backup card's empty state is a bit of a dead end. While we don't want to add action buttons directly to the overview card (to maintain the "Overview = observe, Tabs = act" UX principle), users still need a frictionless way to discover where to actually configure these backups.

### Describe the solution you'd like

As discussed in PR #2114, we should replace the generic empty state with a simple informational message and a "Go to Backups" navigational link. This link will simply switch the user's current view to the dedicated Backups tab.

* Proposed behavior: A text link that acts purely as a router push to the existing Backups tab, avoiding any complex modal state or query params.

### How would users interact with this feature?

A simple "Go to Backups" text link on the backup overview card that switches the user's current view to the dedicated Backups tab.

### Alternative solutions considered

Adding a "Create backup" CTA directly on the card (rejected in #2114 as it blurs the boundary between observation and action and creates fragile routing state).

### Additional Context

This keeps the Overview card strictly as an observational component while still guiding the user to the correct action hub.

issues.title: [RFC] Technical Debt Audit & GSoC 2026 Implementation Roadmap
issues.body:
Hey everyone,

With the GSoC pre-selection phase wrapping up, I wanted to share a quick summary of what I've been seeing under the hood of Montage. Over the last few days, I've managed to land a few surgical fixes (PRs #544 to #553), but the process really helped me map out where the project needs to go this summer.

Whether or not I'm selected, I think these items are high priority for the project's health:

1. **Modernization & Safety**: We've got a lot of legacy Py2 stuff (like `six`) that's just taking up space. I've started adding type hints in #551, but we should really push this across `rdb.py` to stop those annoying `NoneType` crashes. Also, we really need to kill that circular import TODO in `loaders.py` once and for all.

2. **Data Integrity**: I found some favorites leaking between campaigns. PR #553 is a start, but we need a full Alembic setup to enforce these constraints at the DB level so we don't have to rely on application-side checks.

3. **Infrastructure**: The hardcoded debug login in `public_endpoints` is a bit of a security headache. I've got a proposal in #552 to standardize this. I also think we should beef up the `/health` check to monitor the Toolforge volume connections directly.

4. **Juror Tools**: The `entry_lookup` I added in #550 has a lot of potential. I want to build that out into a proper metadata inspector so jurors can see uploader history without leaving the interface.

I've attached a quick architectural roadmap below. Really enjoyed getting into the weeds with this!

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Montage Backend] --> B[Modernization]
    A --> C[Data Integrity]
    A --> D[Infrastructure]
    B --> B1[Type Hints - Python 3.14]
    B --> B2[Remove Legacy Py2/Six]
    C --> C1[Alembic Migrations]
    C --> C2[UniqueConstraints]
    D --> D1[Secure OAuth Config]
    D --> D2[Health Monitoring]
```

issues.title: Threshold logic for ranking rounds is undefined -- advance_round silently raises NotImplementedResponse
issues.body:
## Problem

In `advance_round` (`admin_endpoints.py`), the function explicitly rejects ranking rounds:

```python
if rnd.vote_method not in ("rating", "yesno"):
    raise NotImplementedResponse()  # see docstring above
```

The docstring acknowledges cases 3 and 4 (advancing *from* a ranking round) are unimplemented.

Separately, in `get_round_results_preview`, the ranking round path contains:
```python
# TODO: What should this return for ranking rounds?
# import pdb;pdb.set_trace()
```

This means:
1. Any coordinator who tries to advance from a ranking round gets a generic `NotImplementedResponse` with no guidance.
2. There is no documented spec for what threshold semantics should mean for ranking rounds.

## Impact

Campaigns that end with a ranking round (the standard flow) cannot be advanced further programmatically. While the current flow is `yesno -> rating -> ranking` (final), edge cases like re-running or multi-stage ranking are completely blocked.

## Suggested Action

This should be triaged and either:
- Documented explicitly as out-of-scope
- Or specced and tracked as a feature

before the next major Wikimedia campaign season.

issues.title: download_results_csv uses slugify without null-check on rnd.name
issues.body:
## Problem

In `admin_endpoints.py`, `download_results_csv` calls:

```python
output_name = 'montage_results-%s-%s.csv' % (slugify(rnd.name, ascii=True).decode('ascii'), now)
```

If `rnd.name` is `None` (which is theoretically possible if a round was created with a missing name before validation was enforced), `slugify(None, ...)` will raise an `AttributeError`.

The same pattern exists in `download_round_entries_csv`, which was fixed in #531. This endpoint has the same latent bug.

## Suggested Fix

Add a null-guard:
```python
round_name = rnd.name or 'unnamed-round'
output_name = 'montage_results-%s-%s.csv' % (slugify(round_name, ascii=True).decode('ascii'), now)
```

issues.title: API: Favorite.campaign_id not enforced at DB level -- missing unique constraint
issues.body:
## Problem

The `Favorite` model allows duplicate rows for the same `(user_id, entry_id, campaign_id)` combination at the database level. There is no unique constraint preventing this.

The application-level fix in #540 prevents the cross-campaign collision, but a DB-level unique constraint on `(user_id, entry_id, campaign_id)` would make this robust against any future code paths that bypass the DAO.

## Suggested Fix

Add a `UniqueConstraint` to the `Favorite` model:

```python
__table_args__ = (
    UniqueConstraint("user_id", "entry_id", "campaign_id", name="uq_favorite_user_entry_campaign"),
)
```

And a corresponding Alembic migration.

## Related
- #526 (fave cross-campaign collision)
- #540 (application-level fix)

issues.title: Active pdb.set_trace() in labs.py and bare except in admin_endpoints.py
issues.body:
While reviewing the backend sources, I noticed a few stale debugging artifacts that should be cleaned up before the next Toolforge deployment:

1. **`montage/labs.py:115`** — contains an **active** (not commented) `import pdb; pdb.set_trace()` inside the `__main__` guard. If this module is ever invoked directly on Toolforge, the process will freeze waiting for interactive input.

2. **`montage/admin_endpoints.py:546`** — uses a bare `except:` clause. Per PEP 8 E722, this catches `SystemExit` and `KeyboardInterrupt`, which can mask real crashes during threshold computation. Should be narrowed to `except Exception:`.

3. **`montage/admin_endpoints.py:547,555`** and **`montage/utils.py:185`** — contain commented-out `pdb` imports. These are harmless but clutter the source and can mislead reviewers into thinking debugging hooks are still intentional.

Low severity, minimal risk. I have a fix ready.

issues.title: Missing backend logic: Rank threshold overrides for subsequent rounds currently undefined
issues.body:
Currently across the API boundaries, ranking rounds are permitted to exist in the database, but subsequent endpoints fail to define concrete `get_report` boundaries for rank evaluation vs standard Yes/No votes. In `montage/admin_endpoints.py`, ranking evaluations are missing distinct parsing.

If an organizer advances entries purely based on rank rather than static thresholds, the pipeline effectively passes all entries forward.

**Proposed:**
1. Introduce a strict schema verification on `api/v1/round` preventing transition logic from applying static Boolean/threshold filters to Ranking datasets.
2. Enforce explicit positional cutoff bounds (e.g., "Top 100") dynamically at the `admin_endpoints.py` execution layer to ensure unranked, disqualified, or zero-vote ranking files are correctly culled before the system generates `round_entries`.

issues.title: Performance: bulk_save_objects should replace iterative session setup for Vote generation
issues.body:
During large round preparations, `yield_tasks_evenly` iterates through every `(entry, juror)` combination linearly to append new `Vote` models.  Because `Vote` has numerous constraints, instantiating `O(n * j)` SQLALchemy mappings iteratively into `ret.append(vote)` without committing or flushing generates massive memory overhead for high-capacity campaigns (e.g., campaigns exceeding 100k entries).

**Key concerns:**
- The current implementation forces the allocator to map out everything simultaneously, leading to memory timeouts and slow round creation.
- A 150k entry round across 5 jurors initiates ~750k `Vote` identity mappings individually.

**Proposed:**

In `montage.rdb.yield_tasks_evenly`:
Replace the manual loop mapping (`ret.append(vote)`) with SQLAlchemy's `session.bulk_save_objects()`. 
1. Build `Vote` dictionaries directly.
2. Trigger `bulk_save_objects` configured with `return_defaults=False` to commit the entire list directly to the SQL buffer in a strict subset, eliminating mapping overhead entirely for pure inserts.

issues.title: Inconsistency: load_partial_csv fallback permanently fails during Google Sheet imports
issues.body:
Currently, `montage.loaders.get_entries_from_gsheet` attempts to fall back to `load_partial_csv` if a Google Sheet lacks standard full CSV columns. However, it passes a `requests.models.Response` object directly into `load_partial_csv`, which strictly expects a `unicodecsv.DictReader` instance. 

This guarantees a `TypeError` in all fallback scenarios, which is silently masked by a broad `except (ValueError, TypeError):` block that subsequently forces the code down the unstructured `load_name_list` pipeline.

**Proposed:**

1. Refactor `get_entries_from_gsheet` so `load_partial_csv` appropriately receives the initialized `DictReader` rather than the overarching `resp` object.
2. Remove the silent broad exception catch (`except (ValueError, TypeError):`) protecting this layer so failures explicitly surface instead of permanently deflecting to the `load_name_list` fallback path.
3. This will standardize CSV pipeline flows and reduce unexpected metadata losses when dealing with malformed Sheets.

issues.title: [Feature Request] Display total file counts for rounds in the Admin Dashboard
issues.body:
Currently, there's no way to see the total number of entries in a round from the Admin Dashboard without clicking into the specific round details. 

We should expose the `total_entries` native count inside `Round.to_info_dict()` and toss a pill or badge on the `RoundView` table so coordinators can quickly gauge round size at a glance.

issues.title: Severe SQLAlchemy Import Bottleneck via oldimage Derived Table Evaluation (LabsDB / Toolforge)
issues.body:
Over the last month while monitoring category imports on Toolforge (like Wiki Loves Monuments), I figured out why PyMySQL was timing out and throwing 500 chains. 

In `montage/labs.py`, the queries using `oldimage` views are triggering absolute massive full-table scan bottlenecks because of how SQLAlchemy builds the subqueries. If we swap this out for a paginated cursor or eliminate the subquery entirely, memory and table locks should clear up immediately.

issues.title: Bug: Juror vote state not preserved when session expires mid-round
issues.body:
If a juror's session expires or their wifi drops while they are staring at the `VoteRanking` screen, their entire workflow is permanently dropped.

The frontend doesn't gracefully hold onto the state if the API responds with a failure on save. We should probably drop these selections straight into `localStorage` natively so they don't lose that 80-item ranking order just because of a 401 timeout.

issues.title: API uses latest re-upload author instead of original submission author
issues.body:
For Wiki Loves contests, it's pretty important that the credit goes to the *original* submitter and the *original* timestamp. 

Right now, if an image is overwritten or re-uploaded, `loaders.py` grabs the most recent `img_user` and `img_timestamp`. We really need to update `make_entry` to sniff out the earliest archive record (`oi_archive_name`) and use that instead so credit routes properly.

issues.title: 500 POST Error during empty round creation (Missing Frontend Schema Validation)
issues.body:
If you try to create a new round from the UI but forget to fill out the thresholds or drop a broken CSV link, the frontend fires it off to the API anyway.

The backend obviously crashes with a `KeyError` or `ValueError` which forces a 500 error back to the client. We really just need to tighten up `RoundNew.vue` to trap these edge cases before the network request is initiated.

issues.title: Missing Integration Coverage for Toolforge/Commons sync lifecycles
issues.body:
Just looking through the test suites, there's obviously heavy SQLAlchemy fixture coverage, but `loaders.py` and the actual Wiki Commons Toolforge ingestion logic doesn't have any mock testing.

If the Toolforge schema migrates or something drops on Commons' end, `import_entries` will crash in production and our CI won't blink. We should wire up some basic `unittest.mock` fixtures around the network calls.

issues.title: Middleware vulnerability: Unhandled MontageError bypasses CORS headers
issues.body:
There's a leaky abstraction in `mw/__init__.py`. 

If the backend triggers an explicit `MontageError` early enough in the cycle, it bypasses the `MessageMiddleware` serialization entirely. Because it skips standard JSON packing, the actual `CORSMiddleware` never injects headers. 

So instead of the Vue frontend getting a clean `400 Bad Request` with an error message, it gets slammed by the browser with a CORS block.
